meta.title: Druid: Shape-Shifter's Ritual
meta.pitch: A third-person action-adventure where a shape-shifting druid learns to heal a dying world—using elemental magic, fluid stealth, and rhythmic combat against an eldritch purple corruption—and then managing the reborn ecosystem as it wakes.

pillar.name: World State is Visual and Audio (Not UI)

pillars.pillar: The player reads zone health, corruption spread, and ecosystem recovery from visuals and audio—color gradients, particle density, ambience layering. Minimal HUD, contextual only.
Every input feels immediate. Climbing has cadence. Landing has impact. Combat reads as rhythm—counter windows are tight, feedback is sharp, player feels their timing skill.

Forms and elemental magic used for both combat and restoration. Panther sneaks and clears corruption. Monkey smashes enemies and clears stone debris. Avian reaches high ledges and targets distant corruption sources.
The ritual moment and cascade are emotional peaks. Grey, silent biome becomes vibrant, alive, unique. Permanence matters—returning shows biological recovery.

aesthetics.visual_style: Low-poly 3D with realistic, grounded proportions. Richness from particles, lighting, and atmosphere—geometry stays lean. Strong silhouettes. Readability prioritized over detail.

color_language.state: Soft greens, clear blues, warm haze = thriving, safe

aesthetics.sonic_identity: Reactive music tied to player/world state. Every input has audio confirmation. Ambience communicates zone health (silence → layered life). Ritual music zone-specific (4 element types × potential variation).
aesthetics.ui_direction: BotW-minimal. Diegetic when possible (stamina shown through character fatigue, form shown through appearance). Only essential HUD (stamina, form indicator, zone state). Contextual, translucent menus.

system.name: Shapeshifting/Form System
system.description: Instant snap between three forms (Panther, Monkey, Avian). Collision shapes change in one frame. Velocity preserved. Elemental particle burst on shift.

constraints.constraint: Shift must be frame-perfect, no animation delay
Each form unlocks distinct ability set (takedowns, counters, ranged)
Collision validation required to prevent wall-clipping

system.name: Combat System
system.description: Form-specific combat mechanics with distinct rhythms and feedback.

form.name: Panther
form.feedback: Muffled audio, heightened enemy tells, silent animations
form.core_mechanic: Magnetic takedown windows after silent approach

form.name: Monkey
form.playstyle: Rhythmic counters, crowd control, magnetic targeting
form.feedback: Extreme hitpause on successful counters (celebration of timing)
form.core_mechanic: Counter-based rhythm—read enemy telegraph, hit tight window
form.constraint: Counter window must be forgiving (±100ms tolerance)

form.name: Avian
form.core_mechanic: Tension builds on draw, recoil on release, discovery on miss

system.name: Traversal & Movement
system.description: BotW-style movement with stamina gates and physicality.

constraints.constraint: Instant input response with slight momentum (no floaty feel)
Stamina gates climbing, swimming, sprinting—effort is visible
Landing has weight: camera dips on hard falls, impact audio plays
Stamina depletion follows physics (climbing costs more than running)

system.name: Progression System (Use-to-Improve)
system.description: Skills evolve through use, not XP grinding. Throw fireballs 50 times, damage scales. Parry 100 times, counter window widens.

constraints.constraint: No skill trees or arbitrary leveling
Every player action is practice—mastery emerges naturally
Progression curve: rapid early, diminishing returns mid-game, plateau late-game
Feedback must be immediate (numbers rise, windows open wider, cooldowns shrink)

system.name: Ritual & Restoration
system.description: Zone transformation through discovered rituals. Music-driven timing mechanic. Cascade effect on success.

constraints.constraint: Ritual recipe must be discovered through investigation, not quest log
Timing mechanic: rhythm-based windows tied to music beats
Timing window tolerance: forgiving (±100ms) for accessibility
Success triggers cascade: visual transform + audio layering + particles

system.name: Zone State Machine
system.description: Zones follow state progression: Dead → Restoring/Defending → Alive. Visual and functional changes at each state.

states.state: Silent, grey, corrupted. No life. High enemy density.
Post-ritual, colors returning, audio beginning to layer. Vulnerable to counter-attack.
Fully restored, vibrant, biome-unique. Life present. Low/zero enemy density.

system.constraint: Zone state persists across sessions—returning reveals biological recovery

system.name: Defense System
system.description: Post-ritual wave defense phase. Infinite small waves that scale with zone difficulty. Player choice to defend now or leave affects restored biome vulnerability.

constraints.constraint: Waves are small but infinite—design gates progression by biome threat level
Primary purpose: automate defenses for restored biomes, preventing re-corruption
Secondary purpose: force player to prioritize which corrupted zones are most dangerous neighbors
Difficulty scales with zone, not player level
Player can leave zone before all waves clear—incomplete defense = biome vulnerability

system.name: Ecosystem Management
system.description: Post-defense, player sculpts biome toward self-sufficiency using elemental resources.

constraints.constraint: Player has elemental power budget to spend
Place water sources, seeds, wind effects, heat stones
Biome health meter tracks progress toward self-sufficiency
No "wrong" placements, but some feel more elegant than others
When biome reaches self-sufficiency, player can leave

technical_frame.save_system: Single JSON file (user://save_data.json), portable across machines, tracks world state + player progression
technical_frame.audio_routing: Strict AudioBus separation (World vs Enemies) for stealth-mode filtering

key_systems.system: Swaps form child branches (mesh, collision, animation) in one frame, preserves velocity
Autoload with dictionary-based use-to-improve progression, emits signals on skill evolution
Pre-allocates enemies and particles to avoid runtime instantiation stutter
Wave director and enemy spawner for post-ritual defense
Ecosystem resource placement and biome health tracking

risk.description: Shapeshift wall-clipping: collision mismatch when form changes
risk.mitigation: Implement ShapeCast/Raycast validation before form swap. Revert or nudge player if new form doesn't fit.

risk.description: Defense phase pathfinding: many enemies + dynamic obstacles can cause stuck agents
risk.mitigation: Use NavigationServer with dynamic avoidance. Fallback to simplified movement or async pathing if needed.
risk.prototype_gate: Stress test with 5-10 simultaneous enemies in mid-graybox

risk.description: Ritual timing latency: rhythm mechanic requires precise audio sync
risk.mitigation: Use AudioStreamPlayer with local beat detection. Validate latency tolerance at 60 FPS. For co-op (future), implement client prediction + server clock sync.

phase.name: Graybox Prototype
phase.description: Playable core loop with placeholder visuals, no sound. Prove all mechanics work.

core_tasks.task: Shapeshifting core (instant form swap, collision validation)
Biome management system (resource placement, health tracking)
Rain ritual mechanic (music timing, cascade system)
Minimal HUD (stamina, form indicator, zone state)
Eldritch corruption terrain and visual language
Ritual site models and environmental dressing
Combat hit SFX and impact feedback
Ritual music (core: 4 base tracks, one per element)
Cascade audio (water, wind, rustling, birdsong layering)

phase.name: Feel & Polish
phase.description: Game feel effects, tuning, and visual/audio polish.

core_tasks.task: Combat hitpause tuning (hit detection windows, feedback timing)

phase.name: Testing & Optimization
phase.description: Unit testing, integration testing, load testing, profiling.

tasks.task: Per-mechanic test coverage (movement, combat, progression, ritual)
Particle effect budget validation (maintain 60 FPS at cascade peak)

technical_unknowns.task: Shapeshift Collision Validation: Prototype ShapeCast/Raycast system to prevent wall-clipping on form swap
Defense Phase Pathfinding: Test Godot NavigationServer with dynamic obstacles; validate enemy responsiveness with 5-10 simultaneous enemies
Ritual Timing Mechanic: Build minimal project with AudioStreamPlayer and beat detection; validate ±100ms hit windows at 60 FPS
Magnetic Targeting (Monkey): Prototype lock-on system; test responsiveness, range, multi-target behavior
Stealth Detection (Panther): Implement LOS checks with RayCast3D; validate detection ranges and angles
Zone Cascade Effects: Prototype color LUT transition + particle burst + audio stem layering; test performance
Persistent World State: Design save schema; test serialization/deserialization of zone states and progression data

design_math_unknowns.task: Counter Window Timing: Determine exact milliseconds (current: ±100ms forgiving). Playtest and measure feel.
Stamina Depletion Rates: Define costs per action (sprint, climb, swim). Target: 3-4 second sprint, 5-6 second climb.
Form Ability Cooldown/Cost: Define resource cost or cooldown per ability. Playtest for balance across all three forms.
Use-to-Improve Curve: Design progression curve (rapid early, diminishing mid, plateau late). Validate novice feels improvement, veterans hit ceiling.
Defense Wave Difficulty: Test enemy counts (3, 5, 10). Target: challenging but winnable, player skill directly impacts survival.
Ecosystem Resource Economics: Design resource budget. Playtest: player feels agency, no obvious "right answer".

art_audio_pipeline_unknowns.task: Low-Poly Silhouette Readability: Create 3-5 enemy silhouettes; test at distance. Validate: player can ID enemy type without UI.
Particle Budget & Performance: Create particle effect stress test; measure FPS at cascade peak. Target: 60 FPS maintained.
Audio Bus Routing: Set up World/Enemies AudioBus separation; test real-time parameter changes during stealth transitions.
Ritual Music Composition: Source or compose 4 zone-specific ritual tracks (water, earth, wind, fire). Validate cohesion.
Animation Blending Between Forms: Create two skeletal meshes; test animation blend on form shift. Target: no visible pop or jank.

mvp_definition.description: One complete zone with all core mechanics working: explore → investigate → stealth/combat → ritual → defense → ecosystem management.

requirements.req: All three forms functional with distinct feels
Zone state transitions with visual/audio feedback
Defense wave (5-10 enemies, small but infinite)
Ritual timing mechanic with at least one zone music track
